pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly writes these expected bus names into routing_spec.txt
setup_task.sh explicitly writes this track-to-bus routing mapping into routing_spec.txt and generates the tracks via Python XML injection.
setup_task.sh explicitly specifies these target gains in routing_spec.txt and verifier.py evaluates against them.
verifier.py uses a default of 1.5 when checking the absolute difference between actual and expected gain in dB.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetically generated Ardour session with 6 audio tracks (Kick, Snare, HiHat, Bass, Guitar, Vocals). Verification confirms the instructions in setup_task.sh dictate the creation of 3 specific buses ('Drum Bus', 'Music Bus', 'Vocal Bus') and exactly map the routing and gain requirements (-3.0, -2.0, 0.0 dB) specified in the task description and verifier logic. All metadata claims are explicitly verified against the setup and verifier scripts.
